methods.description: Process for preparing monospecies foraminifera and wood samples from sediment cores for 14C analysis using Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (AMS), including cleaning, chemical pretreatment, graphitization, measurement, and initial data processing.

steps.description: Monospecies foraminifera and wood were selected for 14 C analysis from the > 150 µm fraction from both gulf sediment cores.
steps.searchable_summary: Select specific sample types (foraminifera, wood) from a size fraction (> 150 µm) of sediment cores for radiocarbon analysis.

steps.description: Each foraminifera sample was sonicated in methanol (≈ 1 min) to release detrital carbonates trapped within open microfossil chambers.
steps.searchable_summary: Clean foraminifera samples using sonication in methanol.

steps.description: At least 10 % of each sample was dissolved using HCl to remove secondary calcite (precipitated postdeposition), though in-house tests with and without this pretreatment yielded identical results for these core sites.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform acid pretreatment (HCl dissolution) on a portion of foraminifera samples to remove secondary calcite.

steps.description: Wood fragments from the > 150 µm fraction were prepared using standard acid-base-acid treatments.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform standard acid-base-acid pretreatment on wood samples.

steps.description: Samples were graphitized following Santos et al. (2007)
steps.searchable_summary: Convert pretreated samples (foraminifera, wood) to graphite for AMS analysis.

steps.description: and analyzed at the Keck Carbon Cycle Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (KCCAMS) laboratory at the University of California, Irvine (Southon et al., 2004).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure radiocarbon content of graphitized samples using Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (AMS).

steps.description: We report radiocarbon as 14 C in units of per mil (‰; see Eq. 1 above), which is corrected for decay based on its age normalized to 1950 according to convention (Stuiver and Polach, 1977).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate and report 14C values in per mil (‰), applying decay correction and normalization to 1950.

steps.description: Analysis of a sedimentary standard (FIRI-C) alongside measurements indicates a combined sample preparation and measurement 14 C age error ranging from ±50 years for a full-size sample (≈ 0.7 mg of C) to ±500 years for very small samples (< 0.1 mg of C).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure a standard (FIRI-C) to assess sample preparation and measurement error for 14C age.

steps.description: Because of the similar location of the sites near the mouth of the Gulf of California, we combined the 14 C measurements from both cores.
steps.searchable_summary: Combine 14C measurement data from multiple sediment cores.

methods.description: Process for constructing age models for sediment cores using radiocarbon dates from wood and foraminifera, calibrated to calendar ages, and incorporating stratigraphic markers, using Bayesian modeling with the BACON program.

steps.description: The age model for LPAZ-21P (between 30 000 and 12 100 years BP) is constrained by 13 microscopic wood fragments calibrated to calendar ages...
steps.searchable_summary: Gather radiocarbon dates for wood samples used as age model constraints.
inputs: Wood 14C measurements (depth, age, error) from Radiocarbon Measurement method

steps.description: ...calibrated to calendar ages using CALIB7.1 (Stuiver et al., 2017) with the IntCal13 atmospheric 14 C dataset (Reimer et al., 2013)...
steps.searchable_summary: Convert wood radiocarbon ages to calendar ages using CALIB software and the IntCal calibration dataset.
outputs: Calibrated wood calendar ages (depth, age, error)

steps.description: ...our age models for this interval are constrained using U. peregrina 14 C ages (circles in Fig. 3a)...
steps.searchable_summary: Gather radiocarbon dates for U. peregrina foraminifera samples used as age model constraints.
inputs: U. peregrina 14C measurements (depth, age, error) from Radiocarbon Measurement method

steps.description: ...corrected for a modern reservoir age of 1240 years based on nearby seawater DIC 14 C age observations at 600 m (Key et al., 2004)...
steps.searchable_summary: Apply a reservoir age correction to foraminifera radiocarbon ages.

steps.description: ...and converted to calendar ages using CALIB7.1 (Stuiver et al., 2017).
steps.searchable_summary: Convert reservoir-corrected foraminifera radiocarbon ages to calendar ages using CALIB software.
outputs: Calibrated U. peregrina calendar ages (depth, age, error)

steps.description: ...by synchronizing the apparently region-wide transition from light to dark-colored sediments (van Geen et al., 2003) to 12.1 kyr based on the wood-constrained age from LPAZ-21P... The sudden lowering of Ca/Al at ≈ 65 cm in LPAZ-21P and ≈ 71 cm in ET97-7T is coincident with a decreased abundance of foraminifera and this presumably causes the darkening...
steps.searchable_summary: Identify stratigraphic marker depths (e.g., sediment color/Ca/Al change) and assign corresponding calendar ages based on dating or regional synchronization.
inputs: External age information (e.g., dated wood age at marker depth, regional synchronization age)

steps.description: The Bayesian age model program BACON (Blaauw and Christen, 2011) was used to estimate the age and model error between the age model constraints.
steps.searchable_summary: Run the BACON software to build a Bayesian age model based on all available age constraints (calibrated wood ages, calibrated foraminifera ages, stratigraphic markers).

steps.description: Ages between these constraints were estimated using BACON, as was done for the LPAZ-21P cores.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply the age model construction process (Steps 1-7) to additional sediment cores using their respective constraints.
inputs: Data for additional cores (14C ages, marker depths)
